name: Razorpay API Gateway — Centralized Resilience
scenario: Razorpay has 3 microservices (payment, fraud-detection, notification). Currently each has duplicate resilience code. Consolidate by adding a Spring Cloud Gateway with centralized rate limiting, circuit breakers, and retry logic.

core.description: pom.xml: spring-cloud-starter-gateway + circuit breaker + redis-reactive. application.yml: 3 routes with appropriate filters per service.
KeyResolver @Bean: apiKeyResolver (X-API-Key header), ipResolver (remote address). Both referenced in application.yml.
GatewayFallbackController: 3 fallback endpoints returning appropriate HTTP status + JSON bodies.
GatewayConfig: actuator exposure (health, gateway, circuitbreakers). Gateway routes: payment (no retry), fraud (no retry), notification (retry GET only).
@SpringBootTest (4 tests): 3 fallback endpoints return correct status codes, routes endpoint lists configured routes. All 4 pass.
README: explains gateway-level vs service-level resilience — what goes where.

bonus.description: Path rewriting: /api/v2/payments/** → /v2/payments/** using RewritePath filter. AddRequestHeader for internal auth token. Test with WebTestClient.
Dynamic route management: GatewayAdminController with POST/DELETE /admin/routes. Publishes RefreshRoutesEvent. Test: add route, verify in actuator.
Load balancer config: lb:// URIs with DiscoveryClient integration. Document how CB + LB interact (all instances fail → CB opens).

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. spring-cloud-starter-gateway: uses Spring WebFlux (reactive); NOT compatible with spring-boot-starter-web. Use spring-boot-starter-data-redis-reactive (not regular redis). KeyResolver returns Mono<String>. methods: GET in Retry filter — never retry POST. fallbackUri: forward:/fallback/xxx routes to the GatewayFallbackController in the same gateway app.
